feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 4: How SAP Supports Your Transition
entry.content:
Co-Author: Dennis Leslie

Still in doubt if you should follow the path to convert your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition landscape to the new 3-system landscape?

In the video linked below, Tom Konings highlights the key benefits relevant for dsm-firmenich’s i-Health division to plan and perform the conversion in a remarkable 23 days. He also explains that they can now improve and adapt faster to changing business requirements and are much more in control without any disruptions.

In addition, Dennis Leslie and Calin Cernea from SAP explain why and how SAP supports customers to convert to the 3-system landscape with the conversion program. To help you transition seamlessly, the SAP Activate roadmap provides structured guidance and clear instructions, paired with engaging enablement materials. While good project planning and change management are crucial for a successful project, SAP also provides additional tools like the Cloud Integration Automation service and the test data conversion capability, helping customers to perform a smooth conversion.

Expert talk with HCLTech on YouTube (Part 2)

Explore the power of the 3-system landscape today! To learn more, check out the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Three-System Landscape page on SAP Community.

Missed the earlier posts in this series? No worries, you can catch up on all of them here:

  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 1: What’s in It for You
  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 2: Making Science’s Success Story 
  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 3: Rapid Conversion Strategies

If you liked this blog, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

entry.title: Custom Fields in SAP S/4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
Introduction

In Key User Extensibility,  Custom fields can be added using Custom Fields App.

Custom Fields in SAP S/4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD

Custom Fields is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools                        

In the Custom Fields App, 

  • Create and edit custom fields (extend the SAP table, application, CDS views and OData services)
  • Delete an existing custom field
  • Manage the usages of a custom field such as other Uls, reports, forms, service interfaces, processes, search
  • Define fields of type Text, number, amount, quantity, time, date, timestamp, ...
  • Value help views based on fixed code list or based on CDS view.

Steps to Create Custom Fields

Navigation to Custom Fields App

Custom Fields App

Click on Create

Choose Required Business Context

Add Label name (Identifier, Tooltip appears automatically based on given label name).

Choose Type of a Custom Fields.

Here Phone Number is chosen, System automatically assigns length of it.

If Text Type is chosen, then manually length should be given.

Based on the length of a field, business context capacity will be increased.

Available Types for the field

Choose Create or Create and Edit options

Choose APIS

To expose custom fields through APIs, ensure the necessary APIs are enabled so the fields become accessible.

To display custom fields in the SAP GUI, make sure the relevant data sources are enabled so the fields appear on the screen

Enable the SAP GUI

To expose custom fields through services, ensure the necessary service are enabled so the fields become accessible.

To make custom fields available in form templates, enable the appropriate templates to ensure the fields are accessible

General Information provides information of the custom fields with all label texts

Different Labels can be maintained for a custom field.

Custom field is added in the Purchase Order Header Screen.

Extended CDS View of Custom Field

After Creating Custom field and enabling related CDS View or API, that CDS View will be extended with that custom field.

For every custom field, separate Extended view will be generated.

Select the Extended view, it shows the custom field.

The custom field is added in the Extended view of Purchase Order CDS View

References

  • Before Getting Started with Custom Fields | SAP Help Portal
  • Creating Custom Fields | SAP Help Portal
  • Key User Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal

Conclusion

Using Custom Fields App,

Custom fields can be added to the UI.

Create and edit custom fields and Delete an existing custom field.

Value help views can be added to the custom fields.

Custom fields can also be added to the custom forms.

entry.title: TCode也能快速启动了
entry.content:
在之前介绍2602版本新功能的博客里，我提到了被广大SAP用户熟知的/n和/o的快捷键指令也能在ERP公有云版本中使用了。比如我在搜索框中输入/nf2450，就会打开f2450对应的应用：IAM信息系统：

 

 

         之前，通过这样的方式打开应用只支持Fiori的应用，不支持WebGui版本的应用。但被广大SAP用户所熟知的都是以前那些常用的TCode，比如VA01，VA02，VA03，MM01，MIGO等等。

         现在，我们这个快速启动应用的功能做了改进，也支持WebGui的应用了，也就是那些常用的TCode也能快速启动了！

         比如，我在搜索框中输入/nVA03：

 

         系统就会在当前页面打开“显示销售凭证”的WebGUI版本的应用：

 

         再比如，我们常用的TCode：MIGO，当我输入/nMIGO后：

 

系统就在当前页面打开了我们最常用的MIGO应用：

 

快速总结一下：

  • 搜索框中/n和/o的快捷指令从2602版本开始支持。
  • 除了支持Fiori ID外，以前的TCode（如果有相应的WebGUI应用）也支持了，这对于SAP ERP的老用户非常友好，进而提升工作效率。

entry.title: Custom Logic, BAdi Enhancement Implementation - On Stack Extensibility
entry.content:
Introduction

In OnStack extensibility, Enhancements can be created using Custom Logic App in Key User Extensibility and BAdi Enhancement Implementation in Developer Extensibility.

Key user extensibility also know as In App extensibility, extensions can be added with key user tools.

Developer extensibility using sap cloud abap environment for developer extensions.

Key User Extensibility / In App Extensibility- Custom Logic

Custom Logic is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools                        

In the Custom logic App, 

  • Custom logic can be added
  • Easy exploration of extension points, key user documentation
  • Syntax check, syntax highlighting, code completion
  • Draft, published, testing included
  • ABAP Language Version for Key Users
  • Enhancements can be created using Custom Logi App

Business Accelerator Hub

SAP Business Accelerator Hub provides a section for On Stack extensibility (key user extensibility and developer extensibility), to know list of SAP CDS views, APIs, Business Add-ins etc. are available with the release status.

Navigate to Business Accelerator Hub to find BAdis

On Stack Extensibility -> Business Add-ins

Search for required BAdi.

This BAdi is related to check purchase order before saving to create it.

Here it shows BAdi Definition and Enhancement spot.

BAdi can be used in both Key User and Developer Extensibility as both states of BAdi is in released state.

Methods and its Parameters

Steps to Create Custom Logic

Navigate to Custom logic App

Custom Logic App

Choose Create button to create Custom Logic.

 

Choose Required Business Content

 

List of available BAdi Definitions for the business context.

Choose required BAdi Definition.

Its Documentation.

Choose Step 2 to add Implementation Description and ID.

Implementation ID automatically appears based on Description and it can also be changed.

Choose Review

Choose Create

Click on Publish button.

Its published.

Click on Open Code Editor to write the logic.

Importing Parameters.

Changing Parameters.

Sampe Code -> Show Sample Code

Keyboard Shortcuts -> three dot menu -> Show Keyboard Shortcuts

Write the required logic.

 Navigate to Create Purchase order (ME21N)

Provide the required details and check.

Error is appeared as per logic written.

This implementation class can be opened in the eclipse, and break point can be added in the logic, and it triggers when creating purchase order in the Create Purchase Order (ME21N).

Key-user objects can be displayed and debugged but cannot be edited with ABAP development tools.

Developer Extensibility – BAdi Enhancement Implementation

Steps to Create Impelementation

Create New BAdi Enhancement Implementation

Chose BAdi Enhancement Implementation.

Add Name and description.

Add Enhancement Spot

BAdi Enhancement Implementation is created.

Choose BAdi Implementation to add Implementations.

Add BAdi Definition and Custom Class name

Add Implementation Class

Choose Implementation Class, add Custom Class name and its description

Implementation Class is created.

Implementation Class is added to Badi Implementation details

Logic can be added to implementation class

Navigate to Create Purchase Order (ME21N)

Provide the required details and check

Error appeared as per logic written.

Add breakpoint in the implementation class to debug.

Place cursor on the logic, choose right click.

Break point is added

Break point can be added in the logic, and it triggers when creating purchase order in the Create Purchase Order (ME21N).

References

  • Custom Logic | SAP Help Portal
  • Custom Logic Tracing | SAP Help Portal
  • Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Key User Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Developer Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Using Key-User In-App Extensibility to Create Custom Business Log
  • Implementing BAdI Enhancement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

Conclusion                     

In Key User Extensibility, Custom logic can be added and Enhancements can be created using Custom Logic App.

In Developer Extensibility, BAdi Enhancement Implementation can be created.

entry.title: Transport Mechanism of Key-User Extensibility Objects in Public Cloud & Private Cloud
entry.content:
We are now quite familiar with the concept of Key User Extensibility/In-App Extensibility while working in a S/4 HANA Cloud environment. Key User Extensibility is available in both the environments of S/4 HANA Cloud (Public Edition & Private Edition) and is the preferred mode of creating extension due to its highest Clean Core level and being lightweight.

There are many blogposts available in the community showing creation and usage of the most commonly used Key User Extensibility Objects like Custom Fields / Custom Business Objects / Custom CDS Views etc.

However, in this blog post we will discuss about the difference in approach to Transport the Key User Extensibility objects to higher environments (Dev -> Test/QA ->Prod) in S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition & S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition .

Considering a use case of  Custom fields for demonstration. Although for creation and modification of custom fields, the approach is similar in both Private and Public cloud but there are few differences in approach while it comes to transporting them and one of the key differences being -

A transport process for Key User Extensibility objects in Private Cloud are  associated with an actual Transport Request number while in Public Cloud there is no actual Transport Request number associated while transporting them.

In this blogpost we will focus on the Transportation part but however for explanation I have added brief details on the creation process too.

S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition

S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition

Creation -

Fiori Application used – Custom Fields and Logic

 

 

 

Create the new custom field for the required business context and activate it for the required areas where you want to use this custom field -

 



 

After Enabling Usage for the required areas, click on Publish for the field to be available on the selected contexts.

 

If you want to add this field to your Fiori Application’s UI then you can do that using the option Adapt UI from the particular Fiori application for which you have created and exposed the custom field.

 

 

*Same as Public Cloud in this context
Fiori Application used – Custom Fields and Logic

 

 

The steps for activating the usage of the custom field and publishing are also same in S/4 HANA Private Cloud as shown in the other column for Public Cloud so you can follow all the same screenshots given in the left column for Private Cloud environment also.

**Screenshots have not been repeated.

Transport process -

No actual Transport Request number associated. Transport of Key User Extensibility objects happens using two Fiori Applications –

 Export Software Collection (To be used in Source System)

 Import Collection (To be used in Target system)

 

Transport happens with actual Transport Request number with two Fiori applications used –

 Configure Software Packages

 Register Extensions for Transport

Transportation process in Source system -

The transportation process is done by Software Collection.

The first step is to create a Software Collection and add the Custom field in the collection. This is done by the Fiori App – Export Software Collection

 

Once you create a Software collection you have to add your Custom field or whichever Extensibility object you are planning to move by clicking on Add Items

 

 

You can filter your Objects by selecting the type of objects you want to add in the Software Collection

 

 

Once everything is added you can click on Check and then finally Export

 

First step go to the Fiori Application Configure Software Packages and add registration for your package by clicking on the button Add Registration

 

 

Add your Transport Number from the F4 search help available under the column Transport Request to assign the package to a particular Transport number available.
Optionally you can turn on the Automatic Request handling for this package with the option available.

 

 

Second step go to the Fiori Application Register Extensions For Transport

 

Select your Custom field or the Extensibility object that you want to transport and click on Reassign to Package and select the package you registered in previous step.

 

 

 

By default your Extensibility object will get assigned to the Transport to which the package is assigned but you can change the transport assignment by clicking on the option Assign to Transport Request.

You can now go to Transaction Code SE10 and check the transport number under your name. The Custom field or the  extensibility object that you added will be available in the Transport Request .

You can release and move the transport to upper environments based on the transport mechanism that is being followed in your project.

Transportation process in Target system -

Based on architecture if it is a 3-Tier Public Cloud system then the next higher environment is going to be the Test Environment in Public Cloud.

In the Test Environment open the application Import Collection

 

 

 

Select your Software collection that you exported from source system and click on Check and Import

 

 

If you want to move your Software Collection to production environment then you can move it by the Forward option in the Import Collection app

 

No such similar activity is required here. Transport will be moved and available in QA/Test environment with transport release mechanism followed.

 

**Additional information –

Use the Fiori Application Extensibility Inventory in both the S/4 HANA Cloud versions to check dependencies of objects before transporting.

entry.title: Kick Off 2026 With Impact: Collaborate on 56 Fresh SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now live!
This cycle features 56 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to gain early insights into upcoming product developments and collaborate directly with SAP teams to shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

🗓Registration is open until March 13, 2026.

In a rapidly evolving business environment, early involvement can make a meaningful difference. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can work directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing your feedback on concepts, prototypes, and plans while they are still in the early stages.

To help you explore all available opportunities, we’ve prepared a complete overview of the projects.
View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the topics most relevant to you.

SAP Ariba

  • Reporting and Analytics in SAP Ariba Invoicing

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-powered Code Upgrade Automation
  • BPTX Early Insights
  • Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users - provide feedback on new and planned features
  • SAP BTP Developer's Guide
  • SAP BTP: Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • Validate MCP Hub

SAP Concur

  • Intelligent Audit Evolution: Accelerating Audits with Automation and AI
