  • Trip Extras - Connecting suppliers to business travelers when it matters most.
  • Verify: Shaping the Future of Auditing and Compliance

Data & Analytics

  • Exploring BDC Web & Mobile Experiences to drive user relevancy and adoption
  • Product Insights / Product 360
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • Agentic AI - Analytical Business Insights  
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • AI-assisted task automation
  • AI in Core Logistics (HUM, SN, BM, MM-IM, Kanban)
  • Business Partner and Product Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Customer returns user experience
  • E-mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Improving the Usability of Task and Application Logs in Group Reporting
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Material Long Texts – Automated Content Generation for Product Master
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite
  • Understanding Payment Reconciliation Processes and Automation Barriers in Bank Statements
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

SAP Globalization

  • AI Agents for SAP Translation Hub
  • AI Assisted E-Document Error Handling : Continuous Adaptation

Others

  • Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot
  • How Designers and Developers Use SAP Design System Resources
  • Integrated Planning in Dairy
  • Research into an Intelligent Spare Parts Request Processing Agent
  • SAP Reference Architecture

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: WIP Revaluation in SAP Actual Costing (S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)
entry.content:
This blog answers the following questions:

  • Is WIP revaluation relevant to your business?
  • How to ensure WIP is revaluated properly in Actual Costing?
  • What are WIP buildup and WIP reduction?
  • Example
  • How is WIP in Actual Costing different from WIP in Production Accounting?

Is WIP revaluation relevant to your business?

In a manufacturing business, it’s easy to focus only on the finished products rolling off the line. But there’s something else just as important: Work in process (WIP) - the unfinished items scattered across the production floor. The material components and the labor costs tied up in these in‑progress products all carry real value.

Before these items become finished goods and eventually show up as part of the cost of goods sold (COGS), their value is a key component of the balance sheet. Without Actual Costing, WIP materials and activities are valued at planned price (standard price). Similar to what is described in blog: Actual Costing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for revaluation of COGS and inventory, SAP Actual Costing revaluates WIP at actual price and ensures higher accuracy in legal and management reports.

In summary, WIP revaluation with SAP Actual Costing is relevant if:

  • You run a manufacturing business.
  • Your business has a significant amount of WIP on the production line at period end.
  • The prices of materials and activities consumed for production tend to fluctuate throughout the period.
  • You want to valuate these WIP materials and activities at actual price rather than standard price.

How to ensure WIP is revaluated properly in Actual Costing?

In Actual Costing, WIP is revaluated as part of consumption in Settlement step in actual costing runs (CKMLCP).

  • For materials, the price differences collected from receipts in the period are allocated to consumption and ending inventory proportionally by quantity.
  • Production activities are also revaluated based on consumed quantities and actual cost rates maintained in Manage Cost Rates – Actual (F3422) or Actual Cost Rate Calculation - Cost Centers (KSII).

As you can see, quantity calculation is crucial to ensure correct WIP revaluation. Therefore, before executing actual costing run (in CKMLCP), remember to let the system know the WIP quantities of materials and activities by executing the job “WIP Quantity Calculation for Actual Costing: Event-Based Order and PCC” in app Schedule Inventory Accounting Jobs (F2292).

For any materials or activities consumed in a production order, the system determines WIP quantities with the formula:

WIP quantity = Actual consumption - Target consumption, that is:

WIP quantity = Actual consumption quantity - Planned consumption quantity * (actual delivery quantity/planned delivery quantity)

If the calculated WIP quantity for a material or activity is negative, the system recognizes zero WIP quantity. No allocation to WIP will occur for it.

What are WIP buildup and WIP reduction?

As often seen in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N), “WIP Buildup” and “WIP Reduction” refer to the following:

  • WIP Build Up for Order <order ID>: Increase of WIP quantity in the current period.
  • WIP Reduction for Order <order ID> <previous period>: Reverse partial or full WIP quantity generated in previous period. This part of WIP quantity has been transformed into finished products and delivered to stock in current period.

Note: This reduction is based on a FIFO (First-In, First-Out) principle, meaning the oldest WIP layer is reduced first.

After the Settlement step in costing run (CKMLCP), the following new lines show up for above WIP quantity changes:

  • Revaluation of WIP Build Up <current period>: The price difference allocated to the WIP quantity increase in current period.
  • Revaluation of WIP Reduction <previous period>: The price difference allocated to the WIP quantity that is cumulated from previous period but is consumed into finished products in the current period.

Example

The following example shows how WIP is accumulated and reduced over a 3‑period production process, and how it is revaluated in each period.

Planned Material Components and Activities in Order

A production order (1011600) is created for manufacturing 100 PC of FG228 in period 2025/009. The planned input materials and activities are as follows:

Table 1: Planned Input Materials and Activities

Material/ActivityQuantity
RM16100 PC
RM27100 PC
Activity 1 (Machine)800 min (13.333 H)
Activity 3 (setup)120 min (2 H)
Activity 11 (Personnel)800 min (13.333 H)

You can also view the planned quantities in order itemization below:

Planned Material and Activity Consumption of Order 1011600

Material/Activity Consumption and Partial Delivery in Period 009

In period 2025/009, you withdraw materials and confirm activities for the production, and deliver finished products to stock as follows:

Table 2: Order Transactions in Period 009

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption of Material/Activity

Target Consumption of Material/Activity

Delivery to Stock

009

RM16

60 PC

100 PC * (50 PC/100 PC) = 50 PC

/

 

RM27

40 PC

100 PC * (50 PC/100 PC) = 50 PC

/

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

300 min (5 H)

800 min * (50 PC/100 PC) = 6.667 H

/

 

Activity 3 (setup)

90 min (1.5 H)

2 H * (50 PC/100 PC) = 1 H

/

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

300 min (5 H)

800 min * (50 PC/100 PC) = 6.667 H

/

 

FG228

/

 

50 PC

Target consumption is calculated using the formula:

Target consumption = Planned consumption quantity * (actual delivery quantity/planned delivery quantity)

You can verify the above actual quantities and target quantities in Order Cost Details – Event-Based (F4254):

Quantity and Cost Details of Order

Please ignore the rounding differences in minute-to-hour conversion. Also, activity 3 (Setup) is a lot-size-independent activity, hence the target quantity 2H (equal to plan quantity).

Calculate WIP Quantity of Production Order in Period 009

At period end, you run the job “WIP Quantity Calculation for Actual Costing: Event-Based Order and PCC” in F2292:

WIP Quantity Calculation Job

 The calculated WIP quantities can be viewed in Display WIP Quantities Document (COMLWIPDOC):

WIP Quantity Calculation Result of Period 009

 The system determines 10 PC of raw material RM16 and 0.5 hour of activity 3 (Setup) as WIP for the order 1011600 using the formula:

WIP quantity = Actual Consumption - Target consumption, i.e.

WIP quantity = Actual consumption quantity - Planned consumption quantity * (actual delivery quantity/planned delivery quantity)

Table 3: How WIP quantities are calculated for period 009?

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption

Target Consumption (See table 2 for calculation)

WIP Quantity

009

RM16

60 PC

50 PC

60 PC – 50 PC = 10 PC

 

RM27

40 PC

50 PC

40 PC – 50 PC = -10 PC (negative). No WIP.

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

300 min (5 H)

6.667 H

Negative, no WIP

 

Activity 3 (setup)

90 min (1.5 H)

1 H

1.5 H - 1H = 0.5H

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

300 min (5 H)

6.667 H

Negative, no WIP

View Actual Costing Settlement Result

Unlike other materials or activities, RM16 and activity 3 were partially consumed for completed production and partially for WIP production. Therefore, at the period end, when you settle their price differences in the actual costing run (CKMLCP), the price differences are allocated to “Production of FG228” and “WIP Build Up” in proportion of quantity as shown below:

RM16 WIP and Production Consumption in Period 009

 Activity 3 WIP and Production Consumption in Period 009

 As shown in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N) for the finished product FG228, only the price differences of non-WIP parts of RM16 and activity 3 are rolled up.

Quantity Structure of Product FG228 in Period 009

View Posted Revaluation Journal Entries

After executing Post Closing in actual costing run (CKMLCP), the following accounting journal entries are posted by Actual Costing. Both include the WIP revaluation lines.

WIP of RM16 in Accounting Journal Entry (Period 009)

 Journal entry 4700000057 is for all materials involved in the costing run:

  • Line 19: PRY (Price Difference - Material)

The price difference of 77.22 EUR is received for material RM16 and will be transferred to its WIP account.

  • Line 20: WPM (WIP from Price Difference - Material)

The price difference is debited to WIP account in order to bring the total WIP value in balance sheet to actual cost.

Activity 3 in Accounting Journal Entry (Period 009)

Journal entry 4700000059 is for all activities involved in the costing run:

  • Line 14: PRL (Price Difference - Activity)

The price difference of 0.21 EUR is received for activity 3, e.g. due to deviation between actual and planned cost rate of the activity. It will be transferred to the WIP account.

  • Line 15: WPA (WIP from Price Difference - Activity)

The price difference is debited to WIP account to bring the total WIP value in balance sheet to actual cost.

With that, you can ensure the balance sheet (and P&L) are updated with the real WIP values in period 009.     

More Material/Activity Consumption and Delivery in Period 010

Production continues in period 010. More activities/materials are consumed and finished products delivered:

Table 4: Order Transactions in Period 010

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption of Material/Activity

Delivery to Stock

010

RM16

30 PC

/

 

RM27

50 PC

/

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

/

/

 

Activity 3 (setup)

/

/

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

480 min (8 H)

/

 

FG228

/

30 PC

Calculate WIP Quantity of Production Order in Period 010

Till the end of 010, the product FG228 delivered to stock has increased to 80 PC. The new delivery and consumption in period 0101 change the WIP quantity of raw materials and activities to the following:

Table 5: How WIP quantities are calculated for period 010?

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption of Material/Activity

Target Consumption of Material/Activity

Total WIP Quantity at End of Period 10

009 + 010

RM16

90 PC

100 PC * (80 PC/100 PC) = 80 PC

90 PC – 80 PC = 10 PC

 

RM27

90 PC

100 PC * (80 PC/100 PC) = 80 PC

90 PC – 80 PC = 10 PC

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

300 min (5 H)

800 min * (80 PC/100 PC) = 10.665 H

Negative, no WIP

 

Activity 3 (setup)

90 min (1.5 H)

2 H * (80 PC/100 PC) = 1.6 H

Negative, no WIP

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

780 min (13 H)

800 min * (80 PC/100 PC) = 10.665 H

13 H - 10.665 H = 2.335 H

As we have WIP quantities (of RM16 and activity 3) in period 009, according to the FIFO principle, in the new period 010, these quantities should be consumed first, then followed by the new materials withdrawn and activities confirmed in period 010. Therefore, after you run the WIP quantity calculation job, the WIP quantity by period is updated to the following:

WIP Quantity Calculation Result of Period 010

 Calculation of WIP changes of both periods is detailed in the table below:

Table 6: WIP Quantity Changes of Period 009 and 010

Material/Activity

Current Total WIP Quantity

WIP Quantity at Period 9

WIP Change of Period 9

WIP Increase of Period 10

RM16

10 PC

10 PC

- 10 PC (WIP Reduction)

10 PC

RM27

10 PC

0

0 (No change)

10 PC

Activity 1 (Machine)

0

0

0 (No change)

0

Activity 3 (setup)

0

0.5H

-0.5 H (WIP Reduction)

0

Activity 11 (Personnel)

2.335 H

0

0 (No change)

2.335 H (WIP Buildup)

View Quantity Structure of FG228 of Period 010

The quantity structure of FG228 of period 010 indicates: to manufacture 30 PC of FG228 in period 010, how many quantities of materials and activities were actually consumed.

WIP Reduction

The 10 PC of RM16 and 0.5 H of activity 3 remaining in WIP accounts of period 009 are consumed in period 010, hence the WIP Reduction as highlighted below:

WIP Reduction Shown in Quantity Structure of FG228 (Period 010)

Important: Although these 2 WIP reduction lines are consumed for manufacturing F228 in period 010, their costs are calculated based on the determined price of period 009:

  • RM16 at price 7.97 EUR/PC of period 009 (in comparison, RM16 has the price 0.25 EUR/PC in period 010 as shown in the line above)
  • Activity 3 at price 8.50 EUR/hour of period 009
WIP Buildup and Consumption to Production

Besides the above highlighted WIP reduction, the new consumption in period 010 is divided into WIP buildup and production consumption as follows:

Table 7: WIP Buildup and Consumption to Production

Material/Activity

New Consumption in Period 010

 

WIP Quantities of Period 010 (as in table 5)

 

Consumed for Production of 30 PC FG228 in Period 010 (as in Qty Structure)

RM16

30 PC

=

10 PC

+

20 PC

RM27

50 PC

=

10 PC

+

40 PC

Activity 11 (Personnel)

8 H

=

2.335 H (WIP Buildup)

+

5.665 H

As shown in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N) of RM16 for period 010, the new consumption of 30 PC is divided into 20 PC of production consumption and 10 PC of WIP buildup (if you click button WIP, the WIP Reduction of Period 009 line can show up under Production).

WIP and Production Consumption of RM16 in Period 010

View Accounting Journal Entries of Period 010

After executing the Post Closing step in actual costing run (CKMLCP), we can check the accounting journal entries posted for WIP items.

WIP Reduction and buildup of RM16

Since RM16 has both WIP reduction and WIP buildup, we can take it as an example to examine the WIP-related journal entry lines:

WIP Reduction and Buildup of RM16 in Accounting Journal Entry (Period 010)

  • Pair of lines 17 and 18: Price difference of 77.22 EUR (PRY) is passed onto WIP increase/buildup (WPM debit) of period 010).

Note: This is similar to lines 25 and 26 for RM27, representing WIP increase in one period.

  • Pair of lines 19 and 20: Remaining WIP difference of 77.22 EUR of previous period 009 is deducted (WPM credit) in period 010 and offset by material WIP reduction (PRM).

Note: PRM is only relevant in cross-period WIP reduction.

WIP Reduction of Activity 3

Activity 3 only has a WIP reduction of 0.5 hours in period 010. As shown in the journal entry below, the cumulated WIP price difference of 0.21 EUR is decreased (WPA credit) and offset by activity WIP reduction (PRA).

WIP Reduction of Activity 3 in Accounting Journal Entry (Period 010)

Material Consumption and Final Delivery in Period 011

In period 011, more materials are withdrawn, and more products are delivered as below:

Table 8: Order Transactions in Period 011

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption of Material/Activity

Delivery to Stock

011

RM16

35 PC

/

 

RM27

/

/

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

/

/

 

Activity 3 (setup)

/

/

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

/

/

 

FG228

/

15 PC

The total delivery quantity reaches 95 PC (50 + 30 + 15) and we decide to set the production order to Technically Completed (TECO).  

With that, all remaining WIP from previous period 010 are consumed for the production of 15 PC in period 011. After we execute the WIP quantity calculation job, all WIP quantities of period 010 are cleared up. You can cross-check the WIP quantities with table 6.

WIP Quantity Calculation Result for Period 011

Taking RM16 as an example, quantities and values under WIP Production are cleared and moved to under Production in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N).

WIP Quantity Moved to Production for RM16 as Order is Completed

View Quantity Structure of FG228 of Period 011

As seen from the valuated quantity structure of FG228 in period 011, to produce 15 PC of FG228, all remaining WIP materials and activities from period 010, along with the new withdrawal of 35 PC of RM16 in period 011, are consumed.

WIP Reduction of Materials/Activities Shown in Quantity Structure of FG228 (Period 011)

View Accounting Journal Entries of Period 011

With the completion of Post Closing step, remaining WIP of RM16 from previous period 010 (WPM) is passed onto its WIP reduction (PRM), which is in turn, rolled up to product FG228 (PRY).

WIP Reduction of RM16 and Difference Rollup to FG228

That concludes our example of cross-period WIP buildup and reduction.

How is WIP in Actual Costing different from WIP in Production Accounting?

Working in manufacturing, you’re probably quite familiar with WIP value of production orders. You might wonder how it is different or related to the WIP revaluation we explained above. It can be summarized as follows:

Table 9: Comparison Between WIP in Production Accounting and WIP in Actual Costing

 

WIP in Production Accounting

WIP Revaluation in Actual Costing

Valuated Object

Production order, process order, product cost collector

Materials/activities consumed for production in a period.

Note: Materials must have a price determination 3 (Single/Multi-level).

Definition

Value of unfinished products

Value difference of consumed materials/activities that haven’t resulted in finished products

Calculation Method

Difference between debit and credit of an order

WIP quantity multiplied by price difference

Calculation Formula

WIP value = Actual cost (debit) - Goods receipt value (credit), that is:

WIP = Standard material cost + standard activity cost + overhead cost - goods receipt value of manufactured product

Note: All materials, activities, and products are valuated at standard price.

Note: Calculation for product cost collectors is slightly different than above. See https://help.sap.com/s4_ce_prd_acc.

WIP revaluation value of each material/activity = WIP quantity * (actual price - standard price), where “WIP quantity” is calculated based on all orders of the period with the formula:

WIP quantity = Actual consumption quantity - Planned consumption quantity * (actual delivery quantity/planned delivery quantity)

Calculation Timepoint

Automatic and event-based (whenever goods movement or activity confirmation is performed)

WIP quantity calculation is manually triggered by job “WIP Quantity Calculation for Actual Costing: Event-Based Order and PCC” in F2292.

WIP revaluation is done with Settlement step in actual costing run (CKMLCP).

Period 009 Example

WIP value is:

-1120.81 EUR = (goods issue of 45 EUR + activity confirmation of 391.86 EUR + overhead of 42.33 EUR) – goods receipt of 1600 EUR.

Note: In the accounting journal entry posted by Production Accounting, the value is posted with the following dimensions:

  • Order 1011600
  • Product FG228
  • G/L account: Configured in SSCUI Event-Based WIP and Variance Posting (103940)
  • Reference Transaction: EBWP

WIP revaluation of RM16 is:

77.22 EUR = WIP quantity of 10 PC * (actual price of 7.97 EUR – standard price of 0.25 EUR).

Note: In the accounting journal entry posted by Actual Costing, the value is posted with the following dimensions

  • Order: Empty (sum of all orders in the period)
  • Product: RM16
  • G/L account: Configured for transaction key WPM in SSCUI Automatic Account Determination (100297)
  • Reference Transaction: MLHD

WIP revaluation of activity 3 is:

0.21 EUR = WIP quantity of 0.5 H * (actual cost rate of 8.50 EUR – planned cost rate of 8.08 EUR).

Note: In the accounting journal entry posted by Actual Costing, the value is posted with the following dimensions

  • Order: Empty (sum of all orders in the period)
  • Product: Empty
  • Activity: Empty
  • G/L account: Configured for transaction key WPA in SSCUI Automatic Account Determination (100297)
  • Reference Transaction: MLHD

The following screens are provided for you to verify the WIP calculation explained in the above table:

Cost Details of Order 1011600 in Period

The above screen from Order Cost Details – Event Based (F4254) shows the business transactions occur on the production order 1011600 till end of period 009. Based on that, the WIP value (-1120.81 EUR) is determined by summing up all incurred costs and then deducting the goods receipt value.

WIP Revaluation of RM16 in Period 009

 The above screen shows in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N), the WIP revaluated for RM16 is 77.22 EUR. It’s determined by multiplying the WIP quantity (10 PC) by the difference between actual price (7.97 EUR) and standard price (0.25 EUR) of RM16.

Note: Actual price of RM16 is only available after executing Settlement step in Actual Costing. Before that, only the standard WIP value (2.50 EUR = 10 PC * 0.25 EUR/PC) is available, as shown in Preliminary Valuation (PrelimVal) column. WIP Revaluation of Activity 3 in Period 009

Similarly, the above screen shows in Activity Consumption Analysis (CKM3A), the WIP revaluated for activity 3 is 0.21 EUR. It’s determined by multiplying the WIP quantity (0.5 H) by the difference between actual cost rate (8.50 EUR) and planned cost rate (8.08 EUR) of activity 3.

Note: Actual cost rate is read from F3422 or KSII, whichever is the latest. The Settlement step of Actual Costing revaluates the WIP.

In summary, Production Accounting calculates standard WIP value for individual orders, while Actual Costing revaluates the WIP by using the actual price/cost rate on material/activity level. By combining both, you can achieve a more accurate WIP value, and a better view of how well your production and inventory operations are running.

Conclusion

This blog explores when WIP matters for your business and how SAP Actual Costing revalues it with a cross‑period production example. By understanding the mechanics behind WIP calculation and revaluation, you can turn cost transparency into a real operational advantage.

entry.title: Announcing: Enhancements to Quality Gates and Embedded Launch Activities for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM
entry.content:
Enhancing Project Success: New CQC Reports and Embedded Launch Activity support for Quality Gates

There's a lot that needs to be done, done correctly, and done at the right time for your SAP solution deployment to go smoothly. SAP created the SAP Activate Methodology to guide project teams through the most essential tasks for successful implementation, but how can you check that you're on track as you go? This is what quality gates are designed to do. Quality gates are periodic checkpoints that focus in on the most essential tasks that should be completed before moving onto the next phase. Multiple teams in SAP have come together in 2025 to improve the quality gates, and in this blog I go through the 3 major improvements to Quality Gates in HCM and SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition). 

  1. Refreshed Quality Gate Content
  2. Automated Risk Analysis via CQC Reports in SAP Cloud ALM
  3. 1:1 Quality Gate Support available via Embedded Launch Activities

Quality gate execution is a collaborative milestone, driven by a joint effort between partner and customer project teams and led by their respective project managers. These checkpoints empower teams to demonstrate systematic due diligence and clear, tangible progress toward solution adoption and realized business value.

See the sections below for details on each of these enhancements.

1. Refreshed Quality Gate Content

All quality gate questions for all SAP Activate phases have been reviewed and refreshed. Key improvements are:

  1. All checklist questions have been refreshed to be applicable to the latest SAP concepts, products, and assets
  2. Relevant supporting links and assets have been added to the question documentation (e.g. explanatory links of what the Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA) is have been linked in checklist questions that address the DDA)
  3. New supporting assets created to articulate the concept of quality gates, why they're important, and the CQC reports now available (Quality Gate Overview Deck, Quality Gate Execution - Detailed Walkthrough Deck, SAP Activate & Quality Gate Introductory Video)
  4. Language has been harmonized across the business suite - i.e. roles and terms will be more consistent across roadmaps from different solution areas such as HCM, ERP (Public and Private), Concur, SCM, BTP etc.

The best way to access and review Quality Gate Questions is via SAP Cloud ALM, but you can also view the refreshed list of questions via the SAP Activate Roadmaps accelerators linked here (HCM | SAP Cloud ERP)

2. Automated Risk Analysis: The CQC Report for Quality Gates

The biggest hurdle in a Quality Gate isn't just answering the questions - it's knowing what to do when an answer reveals a risk.

When you complete your Quality Gate checklists directly in SAP Cloud ALM, you can now generate a Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for Implementation Gates report*. This is a tailored advisory document generated within one business day.

*As of 2026-02, this report is available to request in SAP Cloud ALM for the Prepare and Explore Phase Quality Gates for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM. It will be rolled out for Realize, Deploy and Run in Q2 2026 for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM. It is already available for all phases for SAP Cloud ERP Private.  Availability for other solution areas is under development for 2026.

What the CQC Report delivers:

  • Management Summary: A high-level view for stakeholders on project readiness.

  • Individualized Action Plan: Trackable tasks in SAP Cloud ALM to course-correct identified risks.

  • Detailed Recommendations: Direct links to SAP accelerators and documentation to help your team resolve gaps.

By using this self-service report at the end of the Prepare and Explore phases, you mitigate early-stage risks like unvalidated financial structures or incomplete integration designs before they impact your delivery timeline.

3. Personalized Guidance: Enhanced Embedded Launch Activities

We know that digital tools are most effective when paired with expert human insight. That’s why we are enhancing our Embedded Launch Activities - the onboarding services many of you already utilize.

Starting with new customer contracts as of March 1, 2026 we are including 1:1 Onboarding Advisor support for one of your Quality Gate checklists. This means you can have a dedicated SAP expert walk through the checklist with you. The onboarding advisor will be able to support with on the spot clarifications of concepts, resources etc.,  helping the team to ensure that your "Yes" answers are truly backed by best practices and that your "No" answers have a solid mitigation plan in place.

Why This Matters for Your Project

Quality Gates occur at every major transition point - from the end of Prepare through to Run (where they repeat every 6 months). They are designed to be "health checks," not performance reviews.

By utilizing the new CQC reports and Advisor support, you achieve two primary things:

  1. Confidence: That the project team has completed the most essential tasks, is aware of the most essential assets, and knows what you need to know to move forward, or course correct.

  2. Speed: You're more likely to achieve your goals on time by doing it right the first time - and the checklists help to safeguard that.

     

Next Steps: How to Get Started

  • Access your project in SAP Cloud ALM: Ensure your Quality Gate checklists are being completed digitally rather than in manual spreadsheets to unlock report functionality. Follow this detailed guide to begin utilizing quality gates in SAP Cloud ALM.

  • Request your CQC Report: For SAP Cloud ERP and HCM projects for Prepare & Explore Phases, once the checklist is complete, use the “Request SAP Assessment Service” button. Your report will be available in SAP Cloud ALM and SAP for Me within 24 hours.

  • Book your Advisor Session: For new customer contracts as of March 1st, reach out to your onboarding advisor to schedule your 1:1 session for your chosen Quality Gate milestone.  

Let us know your thoughts or questions in the comments below!

entry.title: Migration Cockpit in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
I am a product expert for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. I am delighted to share some exciting innovations from our latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 release. This update brings significant enhancements to the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit, designed to make your data migration projects smoother, more transparent, and more efficient. These new features are particularly relevant for data migration experts, project managers, and business teams guiding their organizations through the transition to our intelligent cloud ERP.

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

Migration Cockpit Release 2602 enhances data migration by enabling deeper exploration of migration objects, leveraging staging tables as structured intermediate storage, and introducing transfer lists for better control and transparency during data loads. With increased file size limits, it ensures faster, more efficient handling of large data volumes—reducing complexity, improving reliability, and accelerating system transition projects.

Migration Cockpit

  • Explore Migration Cockpit
  • Using Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage
  • Transfer List
  • Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

Explore Migration Objects: A New SAP Fiori App for Enhanced Project Planning

A successful data migration relies on meticulous planning and a deep understanding of data dependencies. With the 2602 release, we are introducing the SAP Fiori app, "Explore Migration Objects," to bring a new level of clarity to your project preparation.

This app provides crucial insights into migration object dependencies and their relationships to specific scope items. It empowers your project team to efficiently plan data migration projects by ensuring that all mandatory and optional predecessor objects are correctly identified and migrated in the proper sequence. Through interactive graphs, you can now visualize how migration objects are interconnected. The app allows for detailed dependency analysis, differentiating between mandatory and optional predecessors, and provides focused search and direct access to migration object documentation. By visualizing these relationships, your team can make informed decisions, ultimately reducing project risks and optimizing timelines. Please note that this initial version of the app currently supports migration objects built for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach.

 

 

Enhanced Flexibility with Staging Tables for Direct System Migration

For customers using the "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System" approach, the 2602 release introduces a powerful new capability: the use of staging tables as intermediate storage. This feature is designed to facilitate the adjustment of selected data before it is loaded into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This enhancement significantly increases transparency and provides robust validation capabilities for your selected data. It gives your team more flexibility to adjust and cleanse application data from the source SAP system before the Now, data adaptation is performed within ETL tool prior to loading the data into the migration cockpit’s staging tables. Once the data is prepared and validated, you can transfer the ready-for-processing data to the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit for further processing, ensuring higher data quality in your new cloud environment.

 

 

Check the blog written by Philipp Kreutzheide

Streamline Your Migration Troubleshooting with the New Transfer List

Transfer List

In any complex data migration project, ensuring data is transferred accurately is paramount. When issues arise during the simulation or migration phases, pinpointing the root cause can be time-consuming. To bring greater transparency to this process, we are introducing the Transfer List for the migration approach "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System." This feature is designed for data migration specialists and project teams who need deep visibility into the data transfer process for effective and efficient troubleshooting.


A Clear View for Faster Resolution

The Transfer List provides a detailed view of the data exactly as it is passed to the target API that creates the records in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
This capability is particularly beneficial when you need to troubleshoot the simulation or migration process. For example, your team can now analyze the Transfer List to definitively determine whether an issue lies with the target API itself or if it is related to a problem in the structure or field mapping. This clarity helps you isolate problems faster, reduce resolution time, and keep your migration project on track.

To use this feature, the simulation or migration process must first be completed for the relevant migration object instance. Once complete, you can access the Transfer List with the following steps:

1. From the Migration Project screen, select the "Instances" link for your object.
2. On the Migration Object Instances screen, select the instance you wish to investigate.
3. Choose the "Transfer List" button.

On the subsequent screen, you can view the target parameters, fields, and their corresponding values that were used in the transfer, providing your team with the precise information needed for analysis. This enhancement empowers you to resolve issues with confidence and precision, ensuring a smoother transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Check the blog written by Philipp Kreutzheide

Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

To further streamline your data migration activities, we have increased the file size limits for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach. This practical update allows for more efficient handling of large data volumes.

When you upload your data using XML files, you can bundle multiple files into a single zip file for a more convenient upload. With this release, the maximum size for each individual XML file contained within the zip file has been increased to 400MB. This allows you to process larger data sets in a single file. Please be aware that while individual files can be larger, the total size of the zip file itself should not exceed 100MB upon upload to the migration cockpit. This improvement simplifies the data loading process and reduces the number of uploads required for extensive data sets.

I hope you're as excited about these updates as I am! To learn more, I encourage you to explore the SAP Help Portal and join the conversation on the SAP Community.

What is New?

Check Migration Cockpit Community

SAP Help Data Migration

SAP Community YouTube presented by Claudia Gemm and Elizaveta Kozhevenikova

  

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Migration Cockpit as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Welcome to our Cloud ERP Learning Group!
entry.content:
We are excited to launch this space where learning, collaboration, and real-world experience come together. It’s a space where ideas grow, questions are welcome, and learning becomes a shared journey instead of a solo effort. Whether you ask questions, share insights, or simply follow along and learn at your own pace, you are part of this community.

Every interaction helps create an open, practical, and collaborative environment. So don’t hesitate to join the conversation, share your thoughts, and explore what others are working on.

Before you leave, make sure you click “Join Group” to interact and don’t forget to “subscribe” to the learning community, so you don’t miss any updates.

 

Let’s connect, learn, and grow together. We look forward to getting to know you.  

Hanh Tran (Moderator SAP Community – Cloud ERP Learning Group)

entry.title: Announcement: Register to the new CEI projects for Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In 2026, we start the year with multiple Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) projects for Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

We will collaborate with our customers and partners of the master objects Business Partner and Product Master  to gather feedback on product improvements, enhancements, and new developments to validate our planned enhancements at an early stage, and ensure that what we build meets your business needs

If you would like to join the upcoming CEI projects to provide feedback and shape the future of Business Partner and Product Master, here are the projects that are open for registration now:

  • Material Long Texts – Automated Content Generation for Product Master
  • Business Partner and Product Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Product Insights / Product 360

Registration is open from the 9th of February 2026 until the 13th of March 2026.

After the registration closes, we will invite all registered participants for an initial call to explain the scope and prerequisites of the project. This will be around early April.

Later, we will run dedicated feedback sessions with each registered participant. After we collate feedback from all registered participants, we will host a closing call to summarize our learnings, review the feedback collected, and discuss next steps.

Register today to ensure you do not miss this opportunity to influence and shape the future of Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to meet your business needs. 

We look forward to your active participation and valuable feedback.

entry.title: Configure certificate auth for Microsoft Sentinel with S/4HANA Cloud public edition
entry.content:
Configure client certificate authentication for Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP integration with S/4HANA Cloud public edition

Quick link to GitHub.

For many SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition APIs basic authentication is the default. SAP recommends client certificate use for production tenants.

This article shows you how to use client certificate authentication with your Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP integration. Security Audit Log API serves as an example. Approach applies to any of your APIs governed by communication arrangements.

 

How It Works

Instead of the native connector – which is limited to Basic Auth – choose the Sentinel for SAP extension package on SAP Integration Suite for full flexibility.

  1. Create Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0750 the usual way.
  2. Create a communication user for certificate authentication and upload your certificate. The built-in cert sap_cloudintegrationcertificate provided by every SAP Cloud Integration tenant is supported out-of-the-box for ease of use. For custom Client Certificates learn more from SAP's blog by @marc_roeder and ensure that the certificate signing authority is trusted by SAP. Find more details on SAP Note 2801396.

3. Configure a destination for your S/4HANA Cloud public edition tenant and set authentication to ClientCertificateAuthentication.

 

Property

Value

Description

Name

S4-PC-[SID]-[Client]

Destination name (e.g., S4-PC-YKJ-100)

Type

HTTP

Connection type

URL

https://[tenant]-api.s4hana.cloud.sap

S/4HANA Cloud system API URL

Proxy Type

Internet

Always internet because of the cloud nature of the SAP service

Authentication

ClientCertificateAuthentication

Authentication methods supported by S/4HANA Cloud public edition

Key Store Source

ClientProvided

this will be used as trigger for the iflow to use X509

Key Store Location

(empty)

not applicable

Key Store Password

(empty)

not applicable

This setting is evaluated during runtime on the iFlow. See below Screenshot for reference:

 

4. Deploy the latest Microsoft Sentinel extension package in SAP Integration Suite. See this video for a similar scenario for guidance. Ignore the ERP specific settings on Cloud Connector etc. They don’t apply to public cloud.

 

  1. Deploy the agentless data connector for the Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP. See this video for a walk-through of the first steps and the official Microsoft Learn page here. Ignore the ERP specific settings on Cloud Connector etc. They don’t apply to public cloud.
  2. Connect your new iFlow on the data connector pane on Sentinel to start ingesting SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition logs. On the Advanced section supply the path “/community/ SAPS4_Public_Cloud_Security_Audit_Log” to point the route at the S4 public cloud iFlow on SAP Integration Suite.

 

Observe the messages flowing on SAP Cloud Integration monitoring and Microsoft Sentinel for SAP.

You can use the following kusto query on Azure Log Analytics or Defender portal to verify the S4 logs. Filter by SystemId in case you have multiple connections:

SAPAuditLog
| where SystemId == "your SID"

Continue your onboarding with Analytic Rules

Both SAP’s native connector and the integration suite based approach post data to the SAPAuditLog structures in Sentinel. This way the built-in security content for the private cloud solution is automatically lit up for public cloud too.

Be aware that detections for legacy interfaces such as RFC are not applicable anymore because they are disabled in public cloud tenants.

Final Words

That's a wrap 🎬 you saw today how to elevate the security of your integration of S/4HANA Cloud public edition with Microsoft Sentinel Solution for SAP using client certificate authentication. Good job!

Cherry on the cake: You can save some maintenance by using the pre-provided certificate of SAP Cloud Integration. SAP takes care of renewal. Only remaining task is to update the communication user on S4. This API allows full automation of that step even. See this blog for details on the automatic refresh approach in a similar scenario.

Quick link to GitHub.

Feel free to reach out to talk more SAP Security.

Cheers, Martin

entry.title: We Want to Hear from You: Comment on Finance Documentation
entry.content:
We've made it easier for you to share feedback on our product assistance documentation. Starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, you can now comment directly on documentation for several Finance subareas, such as:

  • General Ledger Accounting  
  • Cash and Liquidity Management  
  • Receivables Management  
  • Invoice Management 
  • Divisional Accounting 
  • Overhead Accounting 
  • Inventory Accounting 
  • Production Accounting 
  • Sales Accounting 
  • Predictive Accounting 
  • Financial Planning 
  • APIs for General Ledger Accounting 
  • APIs for Cash and Liquidity Management 
  • APIs for Receivables Management 
  • APIs for Invoice Management 
  • APIs for Management Accounting and Margin Analysis 

Why This Matters

Good documentation makes all the difference in your daily work. Whether you've spotted something unclear, found a gap, or simply want to suggest improvements, we want to know. Your input doesn't just improve a page — it helps everyone who comes after you.

How It Works

Head to the relevant documentation page, and you'll find the commenting option ready to use. For detailed instructions on how to provide documentation feedback, see Giving Documentation Feedback on SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal.

What Happens Next

Your comments go directly to our documentation team. We review every piece of feedback and use it to make real improvements.

Your Voice Shapes Better Documentation

Ready to make an impact? Visit the documentation pages and share your first comment. We're listening and we truly appreciate hearing your insights!

Related Links

Comment Directly on User Guide for SAP Green Ledge... - SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud – New User Experience Switch Enhanced Explained
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the New User Experience Switch has been enhanced to make change even easier for you and your business users.

Examples of New Version Switch messages

Find out how:

  • The New User Experience Switch makes change easier
  • What’s New in the New User Experience Switch for 2602
  • What you need to do as a business user
  • What you need to do as an administrator
  • FAQ

 

Making change easier with New User Experience Switch

“Progress is impossible without change.” George Bernard Shaw

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, you receive the latest innovations every 6 months. 

With each new release, new apps are phased-in and sometimes old apps may need to phased-out.

For business users that means moving from an old app to the new app that replaces it.

You will have at least 12 months (2 x releases) to make the shift.   Sometimes that 12 months can pass very quickly.  It can help to have a little push in the right direction to get you started.

The New User Experience Switch makes change easier by:

  1. Letting you know a new app is being phased-in
  2. Encouraging you to try out the new app
  3. Warning you when the old app is being phased-out
  4. Giving you a gentle push to move to the new app before time is up!

You only see the New User Experience Switch if you are using an app that is planned to be phased-out.

How does the New User Experience Switch work in 2602

The New User Experience switch was first introduced with release 2502 and has been enhanced in releases 2508 and 2602.

The New Experience Switch consists of a popover displayed when launching the old app and a toggle button in the shell bar that allows users to switch between the old and new app. The switch changes appearance depending on the stage of the app replacement to an Information, Warning, or Alert message.

Information 

In this stage the New Experience Switch lets users know that a new app version is being phased-in, explains its benefits in comparison to the old one and encourages users to try it. 

New User Experience Switch - Information message example

If you like the new app, you can opt-in to start using it immediately.  During phase-in, you can switch back to the old app at any time.

Warning 

As soon as the old app is to be phased out (also known as deprecated), the New Experience Switch changes to the Warning mode to let users know that the app will be removed in an upcoming release. 

Example of a New Version Warning message

Alert

In the last release before the old app is finally removed, the New User Experience switch indicates the urgency for users and shows when the app will be removed.

Example of a New Version Alert message

IMPORTANT: Once the app has been removed, you must move to the new app.

With release 2602, if an old app is being split across multiple new apps, during phase-in and phase-out the New User Experience helps you choose by explaining the choices.

Example of New Version available, warning, and alert messages where there are 2 new apps replacing an old app

What you need to do as a business user

Make sure you use the option to give feedback on the new app especially if you notice something is missing!  SAP product managers are making decisions based on your feedback.  For example, they may delay the phase-out to the next release, while the missing feature is resolved, or new extension options are added.

Do NOT leave giving your feedback until the Alert stage!  By then it may be too late. Silence is consent!

There’s two ways to provide feedback on the new app:

1. Toggle back from the new app to the old app
If you are in the new app and toggle back to the old one, a push survey comes up asking you for your feedback. Especially interesting for SAP product teams are the comments regarding missing features or suggestions for improvements.

Example of the Feedback Survey

2. Use the Provide Feedback button in the Welcome popover in the new app
You can directly access the push survey from the “Welcome” popover available in the new app. After launching the new app, click the icon next to the toggle to display the “WeIcome” popover and use the button “Provide Feedback”.

Provide Feedback button in the Welcome popover for the new app

 

Make sure you use the option to give feedback if you have any concerns and especially notice if something is missing! 

Product managers are making decisions based on your feedback.  For example, they may delay the phase-out to the next release, while the missing feature is resolved, or new extension options are added.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT leave giving your feedback until the Alert stage!  By then it may be too late. Silence is consent!

What you need to do as an Administrator

During phase-In, make the new app available to business users so they can try ir out early, and opt-in to the new app if they wish.

  • HEADS-UP: Starting with release 2602, new apps (delivered for the first time in 2602) are delivered as inactive (due to the underlying IAM apps delivered as inactive). This means, that the new experience switch for these app replacement cases is initially not displayed (even if the new app is delivered in the same business catalog). You need to activate the new successor apps first, before the New Experience Switch is displayed to your business users.
  • You can find more information in the 2602 - What’s New in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • You can find a list of the impacted apps in 2602 in SAP Note 3545277 - New Experience Switch in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Overview of Apps  in the Attachments section. 

During phase-out, you need to plan for replacing old apps with the new apps in your next release upgrade, and remind your users of which apps are due to be replaced.

For more advice on what to do during release upgrades, refer to:

  • SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Identity and Access Management – App Replacement and Deprecation 
  • SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Upgrade phase -  Manage App Replacements and Deprecations

You should also know that once the old app reaches the warning (deprecated) phase:  

  • If you are a new customer joining SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you will be moved to the new app immediately. The old app will not be available to you. This means the New Experience Switch will not be displayed to your users.
  • If you are an existing customer, move to the new app as soon as possible.  If you have decided to switch to the new app for your entire company, you can stop the appearance of the switch by deactivating the IAM apps of the old app. With this you stop the availability of the old app for your users, and this also turns off the New Experience Switch for that app.

For more on what’s changing in the latest release, view the SAP Community topic page for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

FAQ

How do I avoid or minimize getting the New User Experience message

No-one likes too many reminders. 

For business users, the popover is only automatically expanded if you launch the old app. Launching the new app displays the toggle, but the popover is not automatically expanded.

The frequency of the automatically expanded popover differs according to the stage.

  • During the phase-in of the new app, you can use the option Don’t show this again to stop the expansion for an entire release.
  • During the phase-out the urgency to switch to the new app increases to keep you reminded of the need for change. You can use the option Remind me Later to postpone the popover appearance for one month (Warning) or one week (Alert).

 

When is the best time to give feedback on the new app

You can send SAP feedback at any time by using the feedback icon (see below) in the shell bar at the top of the launchpad. Select the icon to give feedback on the current app.     

Good to know: The Switch and its latest features were based on User Research feedback with some of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers – just like you! If you would like to get involved in future changes you can reach out through our SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives on the SAP Community topic Customer Influence and Adoption.

entry.title: The Top 5 Things You Always Wanted to Know About Situation Handling
entry.content:
Situation Handling let’s you alert appropriate users about critical business issues requiring their attention. If you're curious about how it works or need more details, this blog post is for you. Additionally, we've enhanced our documentation - be sure to take a look here: Documentation.

1)    How does it really work?

Situation Handling detects business issues in your systems. The detection works for issues with either business objects or error and warning messages. In the following, these will simply be referred to as “objects”. An issue is typically defined by specific conditions or events happening that are assigned to the objects.  – and then, voilà, either a routine daily batch run or business event monitoring identifies the objects with issues.* It can be, for example, an expiring sales contract, an overbooked flight, and many more. This leads to the next question:

2)    How do I specify the business issues to be addressed by Situation Handling? How is the setup done?

Let’s understand this first: Situation Handling can detect issues from only one object or between two associated objects. If only one object is involved, that’s quite easy – the object triggering an issue, with certain conditions or events, is the very same object that helps to solve it by changing itself in a specific app. One example would be a sales quotation that has expired and needs to be checked for purchase details in order to get back to the customers.

How two associated objects work: One is usually triggering the situation. For example, the situation is triggered because the object is newly created, extends a certain value, or expires. The other object is the one that needs to be changed because it’s the one affected. This is also called the anchor object, as it “anchors” the situation. For example, a change in the processor may cause a purchasing document item to require a review. Alternatively, missing supplier confirmations make operational purchasers check the schedule lines of purchase order items again.

For the Situation Handling engine to detect situations, it needs to know what to look for. The objects with their conditions, events, and possible mappings between them need to be defined. Your business area may have very specific business issues – we got you covered with a wide range of ready-to-use situation templates. They are called “templates” because elements (like conditions) are predefined, while others (like condition filters or your specific situation texts) can be adapted by you. Just copy a template into your personal situation type and enable it.

I can already anticipate your next question: What if my case is not on the list? No problem at all – with our new framework, you can define all parts of Situation Handling yourself. It’s called “extended framework” because of its extended functionalities.

3)    And how do I alert someone? This seems complicated.

I’m not gonna lie, it seems complicated. It’s seems complicated at first, because we’ve provided all the different ways to alert someone that we (and you!) could possibly think of. First, your company’s organizational structure – a lot of customers want to alert specific team members based on their job functions. But we also offer the possibility to alert people depending on the data a situation provides – for example, the creator of a sales order, its processor, or other field values. For more information, check out the documentation.

4)    Let me try it 🤩

Now you must be wondering: Can I check this out? Sure – all SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers have Situation Handling included. Just assign the “Business Process Specialist” role to your user and open the “Manage Situation Types” app. Our situation template “Sales Order Is Close to Expiry” is auto-activated, which means you can immediately check it out under “Situation Types”.

If you want to try setting up something very simple, we recommend following the test script from SAP Best Practices of our Situation Handling scope item (31N)  under “Accelerators”. If you’re more interested in our extended framework, we’ve even built three demo situation templates and our own demo app for creating dummy data. Check it out in this blog post.

5)    Uff… I have issues (and I don’t mean my business issues 😫). I did all the setup, and Situation Handling doesn’t seem to work. No one gets alerted.

No worries at all. There’s already a very well-written blog post that provides a lot of guidance.

6)    What next?

Interested? Great. We have a lot more to offer.

  • Let’s get started 😊 Check out all situation templates to see if any fit your business issues. For the setup, check out Getting Started.
  • Already a pro? Check out how you can improve your Situation Handling by monitoring it and changing certain parameters in this blog post.
  • Interested in AI? Stay tuned 😎
  • Need more help with Responsibility Management and alerting relevant users? There’s also a blog post series available.
  • Start creating your custom use cases (if you haven’t done so already): Blog post

Last but not least: I’m one of your technical writers for Situation Handling. Not only do I provide you with six steps when you only expected five, I’m also always happy to help 🫡This is our documentation (quick link: http://help.sap.com/s4_ce_situationhandling for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and http://help.sap.com/s4_op_situationhandling for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition/SAP S/4HANA) and we love to hear your feedback.

Don’t forget to comment on anything you didn’t get, missed, would have loved to explore further – I promise, we will react! And of course, comment on all the things you liked – then we know what to keep. Thank you 🫶

More questions? Comment on this blog post, and we will try to answer them 😊

@Christiane_Poreski 
@angelika_salmen 
@sebastian_doeweling 
@axel_spriestersbach 
@Dr_Joerg_Rett 
@Vidya_Shetty1 
@NisargaPuttegowda 

_____

* For sure, monitoring business events is way more elegant than a batch run, as it catches issues right away (with some delay due to system load though, but still fairly recent). But obviously, not all conditions can be detected through events, so a once-a-day batch run is performed on the objects to identify those with issues. A batch run also has its advantages, for example, it sends controlled information once a day with no spam by frequent events.

entry.title: Unlock S/4HANA Cloud Secrets: Hybrid Events+APIs Strategy for Seamless BTP Data Flows
entry.content:
Strategic Financial Data Extraction from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to SAP BTP

Using Transfer Pricing as a Real-World Example

Transfer pricing represents a perfect use case to demonstrate generic financial data extraction strategies from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Multinational organisations need GL, Controlling, Material Ledger, and Asset Accounting postings to flow reliably to external engines hosted on SAP BTP—but Public Cloud’s “no direct database access” constraint demands architecturally sound patterns.

Why Transfer Pricing Perfectly Illustrates the Challenge

The business need: Calculate inter-company markups (cost-plus, resale-minus) using real-time ACDOCA postings across multiple currencies, profit centres, and cost elements.
The constraint: Pure cloud extensibility—no RFCs, no custom ABAP, no direct table extracts. 
The solution: Three proven patterns that work for any financial data integration scenario.

Pattern 1: Event Notifications (Real-Time Push Model)

How transfer pricing uses it: GL postings trigger automatic business event notifications routed through SAP Event Mesh to BTP applications.

 

Event Notification

Generic applicability: Works for any posting-driven process—revenue recognition, inter-company reconciliation, compliance monitoring. 

High-level activation:
  1. Integration configuration apps → Activate accounting-related event notifications
  2. Communication scenarios → Configure outbound event destinations
  3. Event Mesh subscription → Point to BTP service endpoint
  4. Payload contains: Company codes, ledger amounts (local/group), profit centres, material valuations

Pattern 2: Standard Query Services (Scheduled Pull Model)

How transfer pricing uses it: BTP service queries S/4HANA nightly for new/changed financial postings.

 

Query ServiceKey services (release-dependent availability):

  • Financial line item services → GL/CO/ML/AA postings
  • Profitability services → Margin analysis data
  • Material valuation services → Cost component details

Generic applicability: Perfect for scheduled reconciliation, historical backfills, or validation runs.

Pattern 3: Hybrid Extraction (Production Resilience)

Transfer pricing demands 99.9% coverage. Smart architects layer all three patterns:

Use CasePrimary PatternTransfer Pricing ExampleGeneric Use
Real-time GL postingsEvent notificationsInvoice → immediate markupAny posting trigger
Historical catch-upStandard APIsMonth-end reconciliationData migration
Day 1 implementationBulk extractionFull ACDOCA baselineInitial loads
Custom calculationsBTP processingException overridesBusiness rules

 Three-tier reference flow:

Hybrid ExtractionBTP Implementation Patterns (Transfer Pricing Example)

Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)
service FinancialIntegration {
  entity Postings as projection on external financial services;
  entity PricingResults as projection on HANA staging;
}

 Pattern: Event handlers + scheduled queries → unified HANA staging → business rules.

ABAP RESTful Application Programming (RAP)
define root view entity FinancialDataView
  as select from standard financial services

Pattern: Analytical CDS views over external data → transactional services.

Architect’s Production Checklist

✅ Tenant validation: Test events/APIs in your specific S/4HANA Cloud environment
✅ Quota management: Monitor Event Mesh throughput + API rate limits
✅ Fallback design: API polling validates event stream completeness
✅ Quarterly readiness: SAP evolves event coverage and API fields continuously
✅ Pure cloud: Zero custom code in production S/4HANA

The Universal Pattern

Transfer pricing proves the strategy works for complex financial scenarios. The same architecture applies to:
  • Inter-company reconciliation
  • Revenue recognition automation
  • Compliance reporting engines
  • Analytics data pipelines
  • Any standard data object/model driven process

Universal PatternKey takeaway: This isn’t “just for transfer pricing”—it’s your reusable blueprint for any S/4HANA Cloud → BTP data integration.

entry.title: 更透明、更可控：SAP S/4HANA 数据迁移 Direct Transfer 引入“暂存表”是什么？为什么重要？
entry.content:
在企业上S/4HANA Cloud 的过程中，“数据迁移”几乎是所有项目里最容易出问题、但又最关键的一环。
如果数据迁得不好，业务上线就可能卡壳，甚至推迟 go-live。

过去，SAP 提供了一种叫 Direct Transfer（从 SAP 源系统直接迁移） 的方式，它可以让系统直接从 ECC 等 SAP 系统取数据到 S/4HANA Cloud，省去了模板文件、人工准备的麻烦。

听起来很方便，对吧？但 Direct Transfer 一直有两个“痛点”：

  • 数据怎么选的？看不清！
  • 想改一点数据？不好改！

SAP 在最新2602版本中推出了一个新功能：
“暂存表作为中间存储（Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage）”
它专门用来解决这些痛点。

下面我们用最简单的方式解释这个功能。

1. 为什么要引入“暂存表”？

可以把 Direct Transfer 想象成“快递直发”模式：
源系统往 S/4HANA Cloud直接投递数据，不停留、不中转。

但业务团队的问题是：

  • 我怎么知道快递里到底有哪些东西？
  • 要是想在上车之前检查一下、重新整理一下怎么办？

所以 SAP 新增了“中转仓（暂存表）”：

数据先暂存一下 → 你可以打开看看 → 想改还能改 → 再继续往 S/4HANA Cloud 送。

这样做带来了两个很大的改变：

✔ 透明度提升

你终于可以完整看到系统“选中的数据”。

✔ 可控性提升

你可以在暂存表里直接调整数据，例如修正业务字段、清洗数据等。
不再是“系统决定你只能接受哪些数据”。

这对业务团队来说非常实用，因为上线前总会有字段需要微调。

2. 它是怎么工作的？

过去：
源系统 → 直接 → S/4HANA Cloud（如下图）

现在：
源系统 → 暂存表（你可查看/修改） → S/4HANA Cloud（如下图）

整个过程被拆成两步： 

步骤 1：Extract to Staging Tables（抽取到暂存表）

源系统的选定数据先被放进远程 HANA schema 的暂存表里。

步骤 2：Transfer from Staging Tables（从暂存表传入 S/4HANA Cloud 迁移应用）

你确认数据没问题后，再继续送入 Migrate Your Data 应用。

重点：你可以在步骤 1 → 步骤 2 之间“开箱检查”。

3. 业务人员能从中获得什么好处？

以下是业务视角的真实收益：

✔ 1. 更容易“审查数据”

以前选数结果看不见，只能依赖 IT 或顾问。
现在可以 打开表直接看，业务参与度大大提高。 

✔ 2. 出错更早暴露

因为能在暂存表直接看到数据，你可以：

  • 立刻发现不该被选中的记录
  • 立刻发现字段错填、主数据缺失等问题

避免到了模拟或正式迁移时才掉坑。

✔ 3. 灵活调整，减少返工

例如你发现：

  • 某条数据的标识字段应该更新
  • 描述字段需要统一格式
  • 某些数据需要从迁移范围中剔除

以前要写规则、找技术人员调整。
现在在暂存表里直接改，很快就能解决。

✔ 4. 有利于跨部门协作

暂存表可以被业务、数据治理团队、IT、顾问同时参考，
成为迁移前的“公共审查区”，减少沟通成本。

4. 什么时候适合启用这个功能？

如果你的迁移项目中具有以下特征之一，建议优先启用：

  • 迁移对象数据量大、字段复杂
  • 业务对数据质量要求高
  • 跨部门数据确认流程繁琐
  • 需要提前抽样审核数据
  • 过去 Direct Transfer 老是出错，但原因不明

5. 常见疑问（FAQ）

Q1：启用暂存表后，我是否必须每次都去编辑数据库？

不是。
暂存表是“可选的、按需使用”的。
你可以：

  • 什么都不改（只用来查看）
  • 或者只改关键字段
  • 或仅做抽样审核

完全取决于你的流程和数据质量。

Q2：这种暂存表和“使用暂存表迁移”的暂存表是同一个吗？

不是！
两种暂存表有完全不同的用途和结构，不能混用。 

Q3：会不会很难启用？

一点也不难。
在创建迁移项目时的 Step 3：Staging Tables（Optional） 打开即可。


并按需为迁移对象启用。

6. 小结：为什么这个新功能值得关注？

一句话总结：

它让 SAP 数据迁移第一次实现了可视、可控、可调整。

对于业务团队来说，这意味着：

  • 更少的不确定性
  • 更少的返工
  • 更清晰的上线路径

对于项目整体来说，则是质量提升 + 风险下降的双赢。

这个特性特别适合数据复杂、对质量敏感、跨团队协作密集的企业。

 

参考链接：SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit – Migrate Data Direc... - SAP Community

entry.title: 🌥️ 为什么 S/4HANA Cloud 原来的两系统客户要转向三系统？
entry.content:
很多上 S/4HANA Cloud 的公司会问同一个问题：

我们之前用质量（Q）+ 生产（P）的“两系统”不也挺好吗？
为什么 SAP 现在要我们改成“三系统”？会不会更复杂？

其实，SAP 推三系统不是为了“复杂”，而是为了让云 ERP 更稳、更安全、更能支持企业未来的发展。下面用最简单的方式帮你理解。

🧩 1. 两系统 vs 三系统，用生活比喻怎么理解？

先看两系统（2SL）：

质量系统（做所有配置、测试）  
↓  
生产系统（真正用来跑业务）

听起来省事，但会有一个大问题：

配置、测试和变更全挤在一个质量系统里，风险集中，升级或大变更容易干扰上线节奏。

现在的三系统（3SL）结构：

开发系统（做配置 + 扩展）  
↓  
测试系统（专门用来测试）  
↓  
生产系统（跑真正业务）

这是一个 “Development + Test + Production” 的体系，也是SAP系统之前如此成功的原因之一。

就像你开餐厅：

  • 开发系统 = 后厨的试菜间（可以自由尝试）
  • 测试系统 = 员工试吃区（必须确保稳定、菜品没问题）
  • 生产系统 = 正式给客人的餐厅（不能出错、不能乱改）

三系统 = 把“试菜”“试吃”“正式上菜”都分开，让餐厅品质更稳定。

🔍 2. 两系统为什么不够用？

SAP 官方给出的原因可以总结为四个词：
发展、稳定、创新、合规。

（1）业务越来越复杂，需要更稳的变更流程

旧的 Q 系统既是测试环境，又承担了变更准备、并行项目等任务。
一旦多个项目同时动起来，就很容易互相干扰。
三系统把开发与测试分开后，单独的开发、配置和测试的环境，项目并行、变更管理更安全。

（2）需要支持更多“开发能力”

三系统加入了正式的开发系统（Dev），其中包含开发租户（D080环境），可做更高级的 ABAP Cloud 扩展。
这让云系统也能支持更多个性化场景，而不会污染测试或生产环境。

（3）测试必须更专业，不能“和配置混在一起”

在三系统中：

  • 配置在 Dev100租户 做
  • 测试在 Test租户 专门验证
  • 生产只运行最终正确版本

这种分离让测试质量更高，也不会因为配置冲突导致混乱。

（4）云升级节奏更快，三系统更能抗“升级风险”

S/4HANA Cloud 公有云每季度都有创新功能发布。

如果你用两系统：
升级期间 Q 系统要停用或冻结，可能影响你的项目时间线。

三系统可以：

  • Dev 做配置和升级适配
  • Test 做回归测试
  • Prod 等全部准备好后再升级
  • 即使在升级期间，也不会冻结传输。例如，D在N版本，T在N+1版本，也同样可以传输。

这样升级更稳，不会影响日常业务运行。

🧠 3. 一句话总结：为什么 SAP 要让客户都转三系统？

👉 因为两系统已经不足以支持企业数字化的复杂度、并发项目数量、定制化扩展需求，以及云时代更快的更新节奏。
三系统能保证更高的质量、更低的风险、更强的灵活性。

🏢 4. 那对企业实际有什么好处？

✔ 业务变更更可控

所有新配置、新开发先在 Dev 做，再在 Test 验证，不会影响生产。

✔ 上线风险更低

Test 环境专注测试，质量更高，减少“带病上线”。

✔ 支持更强的扩展能力

Dev 系统包含 ABAP 开发租户，企业有更多自主扩展的空间。

✔ 更适合多国家、多业务线的公司

三系统可以免费申请并行线（Parallel Lines）环境，可多两个环境（D120和T120），更加灵活的满足项目实施和日常使用的场景：独立的配置、测试、数据迁移、培训等场景。

✔ 适应云时代更快的创新节奏

客户可以在更多测试空间里准备升级，不会因升级影响项目计划。

📦 5. SAP 已经启动“两系统客户升级到三系统”的计划（3SL Conversion Program）

SAP 热烈欢迎所有两系统客户加入三系统转换计划，并准备把所有用户统一迁移到最新的三系统架构（基于 SAP Central Business Configuration）。目前已经有超过50家两系统的客户成功升级到了三系统，这其中，既有来自中国的客户，也有来自全球其他国家的客户。如有兴趣了解更多关于三系统和三系统转换的信息，可参考链接：SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Three-System Landscape | SAP Community

换句话说：

🟢 三系统将成为 S/4HANA Cloud 公有云的“标准配置”。
🟢 两系统将逐步退出历史舞台。

✨ 6. 用一个最简单的比喻总结

三系统=
“试做区（Dev） + 试运行区（Test） + 正式运行区（Prod）”

两系统=
“试做+试运行合并（Q） + 正式运行（P）”

为什么要升级？

因为做菜的人、吃菜的人、正式客人越来越多，让大家都挤在一个厨房里，风险太大了。

三系统能让所有流程更专业化，也更安全。

关于两系统和三系统的区别，请浏览该文档：2SL-3SL-Delta_Features_PUBLIC.pdf

🎯 最后的重点

向三系统转型，不是增加复杂度，而是让企业在云端走得更稳、更快、更灵活。

如想了解关于三系统转换的更多内容或有任何问题，请随时联系！

entry.title: Service in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
This blog shares highlights on Service in SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release.

  • Customer and product hierarchy in service order, service quotation, and in-house repair
  • Non-chargeable service orders
  • Add and transfer customer notes to service invoices
  • Adaptation of OData V4 APIs for service contracts
  • Service order integration with SAP Concur for expense handling

Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

Customer and product hierarchy in service order, service quotation, and in-house repair

SAP introduced hierarchical pricing for service processes, enabling both customer hierarchy and product hierarchy to drive pricing and discounts across service quotation, service order, in-house repair, and service contracts. You can model multi-level hierarchies, assign customers and products to nodes, and maintain pricing conditions per level; standard condition types DCH1 (customer) and DPH1 (product) can be added to the sales area. Activation is done per transaction (BPC Expert persona), with hierarchy maintenance in the BP Master Specialist (customer) and Product Master Specialist (product) roles. Once set, the system applies cumulative conditions (e.g., 31% customer + 40% product) and carries pricing seamlessly across the document flow—solution order/quotation → order → confirmation → billing document request → invoicing—removing the need to re-enter discounts.

This delivers accurate, context-driven pricing without manual intervention, improving response times, reducing rework, and lowering the risk of SLA breaches. Customers get transparent, consistent pricing from quote to invoice, while service and billing teams benefit from fewer errors and faster quote-to-cash cycles. The consistency across repair and service contracts strengthens margin control and billing accuracy, ultimately improving customer trust and operational efficiency.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

 

Non-chargeable service orders

​Non-chargeable service work like warranty repairs, rework, and goodwill jobs is currently poorly tracked, leaving customers without visibility into the true cost and profitability of their service contracts. This hidden, non-billable workload consumes significant labor, parts, and travel time but is not captured in existing reports or planning tools.

The solution introduces a unified non-billable item category that can be applied consistently across all service item types, including services, expenses, externally procured items, stock parts, fixed‑price bundles, and time‑and‑materials bundles. When an item is marked non-billable, it carries zero net value, is excluded from billing, and does not trigger revenue recognition; associated costs post directly to P&L upon consumption or confirmation. This behavior is enforced throughout the service process for consistency and auditability. The model also supports entitlement- and policy-driven scenarios—such as annual maintenance contract coverage, goodwill, or customer relationship management—so service teams can explicitly designate eligible items as non-billable based on automated entitlement checks and integrated NCSO workflows.

Capturing all non-chargeable labor, parts, and travel ensures true contract profitability by revealing the complete margin picture at the customer and contract level. Full visibility into warranty, rework, and goodwill effort improves capacity planning and helps managers meet SLAs with realistic workload forecasts. Aggregated NCSO insights highlight repeat failures and cost of poor quality, enabling targeted product and process improvements. Clear evidence of hidden service effort strengthens renewal negotiations and reduces unnecessary discounts, while automation of entitlement checks and workflows eliminates spreadsheet work, cuts errors, and ensures consistent, compliant service execution.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature. 

 

 

Add and transfer customer notes to service invoices

As a repair-service customer, users want a detailed repair summary (including billing information and instructions) printed on the invoice, so that users do not need further clarification.

This enhancement introduces dedicated Text Note types across the repair flow—In‑House Repair, Repair Quotation, Repair Order, Repair Confirmation, and the Billing Document Request (BDR)—so customer billing-related notes and instructions are captured once and automatically propagated to follow-on documents. During processing, the system determines which text to use based on a defined priority: notes from the Repair Confirmation take precedence, followed by the Repair Order, Repair Quotation, and then notes from the Manage In‑House Repairs app. The selected billing notes are transferred into the BDR and printed on the final invoice, enabling a detailed repair summary that includes billing information and customer instructions without manual copy/paste.

By ensuring the right billing notes flow end-to-end and appear on the invoice, customers get a clear, self-contained repair summary that minimizes follow-up questions, speeds dispute resolution, and can accelerate payment. Internally, teams spend less time re-keying or reconciling notes, reducing errors and ensuring consistent, audit-ready documentation across repair stages and billing. The higher priority on confirmation notes captures the latest work details, improving accuracy and customer confidence while streamlining the handoff from service execution to billing.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

 

OData V4 APIs for service contracts

SAP has adopted OData V4 for service contract management (V2 remains available but won’t receive enhancements). V4 now supports contract change processes via API: renewing items on the existing contract, renewing the entire contract (creating a new one), renewing the entire contract with new items on the existing contract, and changing the sold-to party. Address handling is enhanced so business partner addresses at header and item level can be updated via both UI and API (with similar support extended to other service transactions). Eventing was expanded with Item Created, Item Changed, and Item Deleted, and these events now support custom fields, enabling richer, extensible payloads.

Standardized API support for common contract changes reduces custom coding and manual steps, accelerating implementations and daily operations. Event-driven integrations and custom field support let teams automate downstream processes and capture organization-specific data, improving data quality and responsiveness. Moving to V4 positions customers to receive ongoing innovations, lowering long-term integration costs and improving agility.

 

Service order integration with SAP Concur for expense handling

SAP S/4HANA Cloud service orders now integrate with SAP Concur for fixed-price expense items. A “Concur Relevant” indicator is automatically available for fixed-price expense items; when selected and the order is released, the item is replicated to Concur, and the system suppresses service confirmation for that item to avoid double-booking. Processing can be triggered manually or via scheduled jobs, with monitoring in Business Network Integration/Concur monitors. In Concur, technicians submit a “Service Order Item” expense report linked to the service order, then postings are imported and acknowledged back into SAP S/4HANA Finance, creating journal entries. In 2602 this is a limited, request-based enablement, with planned 2608 improvements (e.g., activation by sales area/company code).

Field technicians submit expenses on the go via Concur’s mobile app, reducing administrative burden and speeding reimbursement. Finance and service managers gain near-real-time job costing and profitability insights while ensuring compliance with travel policies and preventing duplicate cost capture. The end-to-end flow from service order to Concur to Finance tightens controls, shortens cycles, and improves visibility into service delivery costs.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

 

Feel free to follow us in the SAP community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates.

We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.  You can find the recordings of your past sessions on SAP Learning. As a partner, you will find the recording and presentation for Manufacturing as well as other topics of interest via the Empowering SAP partners site.
